tasks.description: Add save serialization guard to useTimelineSave.ts. Add `isSavingRef = useRef(false)` and `pendingSaveRef = useRef<{data: TimelineData, seq: number} | null>(null)` near line 74. Guard `saveTimeline` (line 150): if `isSavingRef.current` is true, stash `{data: nextData, seq}` in `pendingSaveRef` and return. Set `isSavingRef.current = true` at the top of `saveTimeline`, and in a `finally` block set it to `false` then drain `pendingSaveRef` (call `saveTimeline` with the pending data if non-null, clearing the ref first).
tasks.executor_notes: Added serialized save guard in `saveTimeline`: `isSavingRef.current` is set before async mutation work, overlapping calls now stash `{ data, seq }` into `pendingSaveRef`, and `finally` resets `isSavingRef`, clears `pendingSaveRef` before draining, then re-invokes `saveTimeline` with the pending save's own `seq`. Verified by code inspection and passing targeted ESLint on `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts`.

tasks.description: Add immediate 406 retry with version refresh, bypassing debounce. Add `conflictRetryRef = useRef(0)` and `MAX_CONFLICT_RETRIES = 3`. Replace the `onError` handler (lines 105-126): on version conflict, `await provider.loadTimeline(timelineId)` to get fresh version, update `configVersionRef.current`, and if `conflictRetryRef.current < MAX_CONFLICT_RETRIES`, increment and call `saveTimeline(dataRef.current, editSeqRef.current)` directly (no scheduleSave/debounce). If retries exhausted OR loadTimeline fails, fall through to exhausted handler (T3). Reset `conflictRetryRef.current = 0` on successful save (inside onSuccess ~line 160) and at the start of each new user-initiated save (in the setTimeout callback of `scheduleSave` ~line 193, before calling saveTimeline). Note: `saveMutation.onError` is synchronous but we need async for loadTimeline — restructure: move error handling into the `saveTimeline` try/catch around `mutateAsync` instead of relying on useMutation's `onError`.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `MAX_CONFLICT_RETRIES` and `conflictRetryRef`, removed the synchronous `useMutation.onError`, and moved conflict handling into `saveTimeline`'s async `catch`. On 406, the hook now awaits `provider.loadTimeline(timelineId)`, updates `configVersionRef.current`, increments `conflictRetryRef.current`, and immediately re-enters `saveTimeline(..., { bypassQueue: true })` instead of scheduling through debounce. Successful saves reset `conflictRetryRef.current = 0`, and the debounced user save path also resets it before invoking `saveTimeline`. Verified by targeted code inspection and passing ESLint on `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts`.

tasks.description: Handle retries-exhausted fallback per FLAG-001. When all 3 conflict retries fail (or loadTimeline fails during retry), do NOT silently force `savedSeqRef.current = editSeqRef.current`. Instead: (1) set `saveStatus` to `'error'`, (2) expose a callback or state flag (e.g., `onConflictExhausted`) that the existing conflict dialog in VideoEditorShell.tsx (line 451-464) can consume. The dialog already offers 'Keep local draft' (which should re-attempt save with fresh version) and 'Discard and reload' (which calls `reloadFromServer`). Wire the exhausted state to open this dialog. This preserves the LWW-with-user-consent contract and avoids silently discarding local edits.
tasks.executor_notes: Added explicit exhausted-conflict state and recovery plumbing. `useTimelineSave.ts` now sets `isConflictExhausted` plus `saveStatus = 'error'` on exhausted/load-failed conflicts without forcing `savedSeqRef`, exposes `retrySaveAfterConflict()` to reload the current version and retry the local draft, clears the exhausted state on successful save/reload, and keeps `reloadFromServer()` as the discard path. That state/callback is threaded through `useTimelineData.ts`, `useTimelineState.types.ts`, and `useTimelineState.ts`, then consumed by `useTimelineRealtime.ts` and `VideoEditorShell.tsx` so the existing dialog opens from explicit exhausted-conflict state and its 'Keep local draft' action now re-attempts save with a refreshed version. Verified by targeted code inspection and passing ESLint on all touched files.

tasks.description: Handle pending save drain after conflict retry success. After any successful save (including conflict retries), check `pendingSaveRef` in the `finally` block. If a newer save was queued during the retry cycle, drain it. Important: compare the pending seq against the just-saved seq — only drain if pending data is actually newer (pendingSaveRef.current.seq > seq that just saved). Clear pendingSaveRef before calling saveTimeline to avoid re-entrancy issues. This addresses FLAG-002 (avoid resubmitting same snapshot).
tasks.executor_notes: Updated the serialized drain path in `useTimelineSave.ts` so successful save cycles carry a shared `completedSeqRef`, clear `pendingSaveRef` before draining, and only re-run a queued save when `pendingSave.seq > completedSeqRef.current`. This prevents re-submitting an already-persisted snapshot after conflict-retry success while preserving pending-save drain behavior when no save completed. Verified by targeted inspection of the save/finally path and passing ESLint.

tasks.description: Add dev-only logging for conflict recovery. Add `console.log('[TimelineSave] ...')` statements: (1) when 406 detected — log expected vs actual version, (2) when retry fires — log attempt number, (3) when retry succeeds — log final version, (4) when retries exhausted — log warning. Per project convention, debug logs are stripped in prod so bare console.log with [TimelineSave] tag is fine.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `[TimelineSave]` logs in `useTimelineSave.ts` for conflict detection (`expectedVersion`/`actualVersion`), retry firing (`attempt`, `expectedVersion`, `actualVersion`), retry success (`attempts`, `finalVersion`), and retry exhaustion (`expectedVersion`, `actualVersion` when known, `retries`, `reason`). Verified by targeted inspection of the conflict-handling branch and passing ESLint.

tasks.description: Manual validation. (1) Open timeline editor, rapidly move clips. Verify save status cycles dirty→saving→saved without sticking on error. (2) Open two tabs on same timeline, edit in both — both should reach 'saved'. (3) Verify polling still syncs external changes within 30s. (4) Verify normal editing (add/move/delete clips, effects) saves without errors. (5) Verify the conflict dialog appears when retries are exhausted and both options work correctly.

watch_items: FLAG-001 (significant): Do NOT silently force savedSeqRef when retries exhaust — wire into existing conflict dialog (VideoEditorShell.tsx:451-464) so user chooses 'Keep local draft' or 'Discard and reload'
FLAG-002 (minor): Pending save drain must compare seq numbers to avoid resubmitting an already-saved snapshot, which would cause unnecessary version bumps and new conflicts

FLAG-003 (minor): This adds a new serialization mechanism (isSavingRef + pendingSaveRef). If a serialized write queue already exists elsewhere in the codebase, prefer reusing it. Explore agent found none, but verify during implementation.
FLAG-004 (minor): No automated tests specified. If test scaffolding exists for timeline save logic, add tests. If not, manual testing (T6) is the validation.
DEBT-conflict-resolution: Two existing debt entries in this subsystem. This fix should reduce conflict frequency (serialization) and add user-consent recovery (dialog). Do not add a third debt item.
DECISION-001: Last-write-wins with local priority. No three-way merge. This is settled.
DECISION-002: Save serialization via isSavingRef, not react-query mutation state. Manual control needed for retry bypass.
DECISION-003: 406 retry bypasses debounce and calls saveTimeline directly. The debounce timer is the root cause of deadlock.

The onError handler in useMutation is synchronous — but the retry needs async loadTimeline. Restructure: catch the error from mutateAsync in saveTimeline's try/catch instead of using useMutation's onError callback.
Normal debounce (500ms) must still work for regular edits. Only 406 retries bypass it.

The saveMutation.mutateAsync call currently has inline onSuccess — this will need restructuring when moving error handling to try/catch. Keep onSuccess logic (version update, savedSeqRef, setSaveStatus) intact.

sense_checks.question: Does the serialization guard correctly prevent overlapping saveTimeline calls while still draining pending saves? Verify: (a) isSavingRef is set true before any async work and false in finally, (b) pendingSaveRef is cleared before the drain call to prevent infinite loops, (c) the drain call uses the pending data's seq, not a stale editSeqRef.
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified the serialization guard behavior in `useTimelineSave.ts`: (a) `isSavingRef.current = true` is set before `mutateAsync` and reset in `finally`; (b) `pendingSaveRef.current` is nulled before the drain call; (c) the drain path calls `saveTimeline(pendingSave.data, pendingSave.seq)`, so it uses the queued save's `seq` rather than `editSeqRef.current`.

sense_checks.question: Does the 406 retry correctly bypass debounce? Verify: (a) retry calls saveTimeline directly, not scheduleSave, (b) conflictRetryRef resets to 0 on success AND on new user-initiated saves, (c) the loadTimeline version refresh actually updates configVersionRef before the retry save fires.
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified the retry path bypasses debounce: (a) 406 recovery now retries from inside `saveTimeline` via `await saveTimeline(dataRef.current, editSeqRef.current, { bypassQueue: true })`, with no `scheduleSave` call on the conflict path; (b) `conflictRetryRef.current` resets to `0` in the mutation success handler and again in the `scheduleSave` timeout before a new debounced save starts; (c) the conflict branch awaits `provider.loadTimeline(timelineId)` and sets `configVersionRef.current = loaded.configVersion` before the retry save is invoked.

sense_checks.question: When retries exhaust, does the conflict dialog surface correctly? Verify: (a) savedSeqRef is NOT force-updated (FLAG-001), (b) the dialog opens with correct options, (c) 'Keep local draft' re-attempts save with fresh version, (d) 'Discard and reload' calls reloadFromServer which resets editSeqRef and savedSeqRef.
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified the exhausted-conflict path now uses explicit conflict state instead of forcing `savedSeqRef`: `handleConflictExhausted` only sets `isConflictExhausted` and `saveStatus = 'error'`, with no `savedSeqRef` override. `VideoEditorShell` passes `chrome.isConflictExhausted` plus `chrome.retrySaveAfterConflict` into `useTimelineRealtime`, so the existing dialog opens from that state. 'Keep local draft' now calls `retrySaveAfterConflict()`, which refreshes `configVersionRef` from `loadTimeline()` before re-invoking `saveTimeline`, and 'Discard and reload' still calls `reloadFromServer()`, which clears exhausted state, drops pending saves, and resets the local dirty/edit state back to the server snapshot.

sense_checks.question: Does pending save drain avoid resubmitting stale data? Verify: (a) seq comparison prevents saving already-persisted state, (b) drain only fires after isSavingRef is reset to false (or the drain itself correctly re-sets it to true), (c) no race between drain and a new user edit arriving simultaneously.
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified the pending-save drain no longer resubmits already-saved data. `saveTimeline` now carries a shared `completedSeqRef`; on successful save it records the exact saved `seq`, and the outer `finally` clears `pendingSaveRef` before draining and only drains when `pendingSave.seq > completedSeqRef.current`. The drain still runs only after `isSavingRef.current` is reset to `false` on the outer save cycle, so the next queued save re-enters through the normal guard and does not race with the in-flight attempt.

sense_checks.question: Are all log statements tagged [TimelineSave] and do they include actionable data (expected/actual version, retry count, final version)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified all added conflict-recovery logs are tagged `[TimelineSave]`. The conflict-detected log includes `expectedVersion` and `actualVersion`; the retry-fire log includes `attempt`, `expectedVersion`, and `actualVersion`; the retry-success log includes `attempts` and `finalVersion`; and the exhausted log includes retry count plus version context when available (`expectedVersion`, `actualVersion`, `retries`, `reason`).

sense_checks.question: Do all five success criteria pass? (1) Rapid clip moves recover to 'saved', (2) two-tab concurrent edits both save, (3) polling resumes after conflict, (4) serialization prevents overlapping saves, (5) debounce still batches normal rapid edits.
sense_checks.executor_note: I cannot confirm that all five success criteria pass from this environment. Criteria (1) through (5) are manual editor behaviors that require an interactive browser session with the timeline UI, and this workspace does not include a runnable E2E harness for them. Supporting automated checks did pass: the existing `timeline-save-utils` Vitest suite passed (5/5), and targeted ESLint passed on the touched save/realtime/UI files. Manual confirmation for rapid clip moves, two-tab saves, polling sync, overlap prevention in the live editor, debounce behavior, and the exhausted-conflict dialog remains pending.

meta_commentary:
Key execution guidance:

**Restructuring onError → try/catch**: The biggest structural change is moving error handling out of `useMutation({ onError })` and into a try/catch around `saveMutation.mutateAsync()` inside `saveTimeline`. This is necessary because the retry path needs `await provider.loadTimeline()` which can't run in the synchronous `onError` callback. When restructuring, preserve the existing non-conflict error retry (lines 123-125) and the onSuccess logic (lines 160-178).

**Serialization + retry interaction**: The serialization guard (T1) and retry mechanism (T2) interact. When a 406 retry fires, it calls `saveTimeline` recursively. Since `isSavingRef` is still `true` during the retry, the recursive call must NOT be blocked by the guard. Solution: the retry happens inside `saveTimeline` itself (before the `finally` block), so `isSavingRef` stays `true` throughout the retry cycle. The `finally` block only runs after the last retry succeeds or fails.

**FLAG-001 is the most important correctness concern**: The plan's Step 3 proposed `savedSeqRef.current = editSeqRef.current` on exhaustion, which would let polling silently overwrite local edits. Instead, surface the existing conflict dialog. The `useTimelineRealtime` hook (referenced at VideoEditorShell.tsx:66-69) already manages conflict state — check if it can be extended, or expose a new callback from `useTimelineSave` that the shell wires into a similar dialog.

**Execution order**: T1 first (reduces 406 frequency, simplifies reasoning). T2+T4 together (retry + drain form one mechanism). T3 separately (correctness-critical, needs careful wiring to UI). T5 last (independent). T6 after all code.
